title: 60 Nations Backing Fossil Fuel Exit ‘A New Power’
description: Major fossil fuel producers are joining the first-of-its-kind talks, but the biggest greenhouse gas emitters -- including the United States, China, and Russia -- are skipping the event. The post 60 Nations Backing Fossil Fuel Exit ‘A New Power’ appeared first on Channels Television .

title: More than 50 countries, including Australia, meet to thrash out an orderly path away from fossil fuels
description: Energy minister Chris Bowen is absent from talks in Colombia, but has been voicing support for the clean energy transition as a long-term pathway towards energy security. The post More than 50 countries, including Australia, meet to thrash out an orderly path away from fossil fuels appeared first on Renew Economy.

title: Nations backing fossil fuel exit ‘a new power’: conference host Colombia
description: Around 60 nations are attending a world-first conference in Colombia to tackle an issue that has deadlocked the UN climate talks — how to exit the fossil fuels that cause global warming. Colombia’s Environment Minister Irene Velez Torres has been prominent as host of this breakaway climate conference, which has drawn nations wanting to accelerate [...] The post Nations backing fossil fuel exit ‘a new power’: conference host Colombia appeared first on Digital Journal .
